g1: Loss of appetite without altered eating habits
g2: Oral intake decreased without significant weight loss, dehydration, or malnutrition
g3: Inadequate oral intake; tube feeding, TPN, or hospitalization indicated
definition: Sensation of unease and discomfort in the upper stomach with urge to vomit.

g1: Occasional or intermittent symptoms; occasional stool softener or laxative indicated
g2: Persistent symptoms with regular laxatives or enemas indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
g3: Obstipation with manual evacuation indicated; limiting self-care ADL
definition: Infrequent or difficult evacuation of feces.
note: Consider ileus or bowel obstruction if severe.

g3: Severe pain; limiting self-care ADL
note: Broad symptom term; search etiology separately.

g3: Transfusion, radiologic, endoscopic, or operative intervention indicated
definition: Bleeding originating in the gastrointestinal tract.
note: Consider anti-VEGF, thrombocytopenia and tumor bleeding.

g1: Asymptomatic enzyme elevation or mild symptoms
g3: Severe pain, vomiting, or hospitalization indicated
Severe symptoms; invasive intervention indicated
definition: Accumulation of fluid in the peritoneal cavity.
note: Useful for liver cancer and peritoneal disease.

g3: Severe sodium decrease or hospitalization indicated

g2: Moderate potassium decrease; oral replacement indicated
g3: Severe potassium decrease; IV replacement or hospitalization indicated
note: Relevant for diarrhea, vomiting and renal toxicity.

g3: Severe hyperglycemia; hospitalization indicated
g4: Life-threatening consequences, ketoacidosis, or urgent intervention indicated
note: Important for steroids and ICI-related diabetes.

g3: IV fluids or hospitalization indicated >24 hours
note: Common with diarrhea, vomiting and poor intake.

g2: 10 to <20% from baseline; nutritional support indicated
g3: >=20% from baseline; tube feeding or TPN indicated

g2: Moderate symptoms; IV antibiotics or oxygen indicated
g3: Severe symptoms; hospitalization indicated
Severe systemic infection; hospitalization indicated
g4: Life-threatening consequences; vasopressor or ICU-level care indicated

g3: Severe skin/mucosal eruption; hospitalization indicated

g1: Papules/pustules covering <10% BSA; symptoms absent or mild
g2: 10-30% BSA or psychosocial impact; intervention indicated
g3: >30% BSA or limiting self-care ADL; oral antibiotics indicated
Medical intervention or hospitalization indicated
note: Consider infusion reaction, sepsis and adrenal crisis.

g3: Symptomatic and incompletely controlled; hospitalization indicated
definition: Irregular atrial rhythm consistent with atrial fibrillation.
note: Check electrolytes, infection and cardiac toxicity.

g2: Asymptomatic decline requiring intervention or increased monitoring
g3: Symptomatic heart failure or severe decline; intervention indicated
definition: Decrease in left ventricular ejection fraction.
note: Important for HER2 agents, TKI and anthracycline.

g1: Mild cognitive symptoms; not limiting ADL
g3: Severe symptoms; limiting self-care ADL

g1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; lubricants indicated
g3: Severe symptoms; limiting self-care ADL or urgent ophthalmology indicated
Symptomatic bronchospasm, angioedema, or parenteral intervention indicated
